refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication packet loss at or below the customer-defined threshold (acceptance criterion: ≤1% under agreed network conditions) to ensure safe and effective remote equipment operation.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-009 addresses a critical quality-of-service requirement for teleoperation. Packet loss directly impacts operator situational awareness, control responsiveness, and safety. Refining the FR to explicitly reference the acceptance criterion (≤1%) and network conditions scope clarifies the verification boundary and prevents ambiguity in acceptance testing.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct packet loss measurement under representative network conditions (latency, jitter, bandwidth constraints) agreed with customer. Measure end-to-end packet loss across the teleoperation communication path (operator input → vehicle → operator feedback) over sustained operational periods. Compare measured loss against customer-defined threshold.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-009.1: The teleoperation system shall transmit operator control commands with reliable delivery to the remote vehicle.
FR-NGHE-009.2: The teleoperation system shall transmit vehicle telemetry and sensor data to the operator with reliable delivery.
FR-NGHE-009.3: The teleoperation system shall implement error detection and recovery mechanisms to minimize packet loss impact on safety-critical communications.
FR-NGHE-009.4: The teleoperation system shall monitor and report communication quality metrics (packet loss, latency) to enable operator awareness and system diagnostics.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement redundant communication channels (dual network paths) with automatic failover to reduce single-point-of-failure packet loss.
DP-B: Deploy adaptive quality-of-service (QoS) prioritization and packet scheduling to minimize loss of critical control and safety messages.
DP-C: Integrate forward error correction (FEC) and retransmission protocols at the application layer to recover lost packets.
DP-D: Combine network-level redundancy (DP-A), QoS prioritization (DP-B), and application-layer recovery (DP-C) in a layered architecture.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Implement a layered packet loss mitigation architecture combining (1) redundant communication channels with automatic failover, (2) QoS-based prioritization of safety-critical and control messages, and (3) application-layer forward error correction and selective retransmission for critical telemetry.

potential_sources: Coupling between redundancy logic (DP-A) and QoS scheduler (DP-B) requires coordinated state management to avoid duplicate message delivery.
Coupling between application-layer FEC (DP-C) and network-layer redundancy (DP-A) requires clear demarcation of responsibility to prevent over-correction.
Coupling with Safety Services module for safety-critical message prioritization and validation.
coupling_check.notes: Layered approach is well-established in telecommunications and avionics. Coupling is manageable through clear interface contracts and message classification schemes. Recommend explicit safety message tagging and priority levels to decouple QoS logic from safety domain knowledge.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Customer-defined network conditions are stable and representative of actual deployment environment. Risk: If actual field conditions differ significantly, packet loss targets may not be achievable.

Assumption: Redundant communication channels (e.g., dual cellular, satellite backup) are available and cost-effective. Risk: Availability and cost may vary by geography; may not be feasible in all deployment regions.

Assumption: Application-layer FEC and retransmission do not introduce unacceptable latency. Risk: Retransmission delays could violate operator responsiveness requirements; FEC overhead may consume bandwidth.

Assumption: Safety-critical message classification is well-defined and stable. Risk: Incomplete or incorrect classification could result in non-critical messages receiving safety-level protection (waste) or safety messages being deprioritized (hazard).
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-D to explicitly specify: (1) message classification scheme (safety-critical, control, telemetry, diagnostic), (2) redundancy strategy (e.g., active-active vs. active-passive), (3) FEC parameters (code rate, block size), (4) retransmission timeout and retry limits, and (5) latency budget allocation across layers. Recommend trade study to validate that combined latency (redundancy switching + FEC decoding + retransmission) remains within operator responsiveness requirements (typically <200ms for heavy equipment teleoperation).

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: The Teleoperation module owns the end-to-end communication pipeline for remote equipment operation, including operator interface, command transmission, vehicle-side reception and execution, telemetry collection, and feedback transmission back to operator. This module is responsible for implementing packet loss mitigation strategies (redundancy, QoS, FEC, retransmission) and exposing communication quality metrics.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operator control commands and mission directives from the operator console/interface. Tasking carries control inputs (steering, throttle, bucket position, etc.) that must be transmitted to the vehicle with minimal packet loss.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Transmits vehicle state, sensor readings, and operational status back to the operator. Telemetry includes position, orientation, load, fuel, temperature, and diagnostic data. Packet loss on Telemetry degrades operator situational awareness.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Abstracts the underlying communication medium (cellular, satellite, WiFi, wired). Teleoperation module uses Network Link to transmit Tasking commands and receive Telemetry. Network Link may exhibit packet loss; Teleoperation mitigates this loss.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Transmits safety-critical alerts and emergency stop signals to the operator. SafetyAlert messages must be prioritized and protected against packet loss to ensure operator can respond to hazardous conditions.

module_containment_scores.notes: Teleoperation is the natural owner of packet loss mitigation for teleoperation communication. All DP-D components (redundancy, QoS, FEC, retransmission) are logically contained within the teleoperation communication pipeline. Minimal diffusion: only interfaces to Network Link (for redundant channel access) and Safety Services (for safety message prioritization).

Vehicle module could own vehicle-side reception and retransmission logic, but this would split the teleoperation communication pipeline across two modules, creating high diffusion. Vehicle should not own operator-side command transmission or feedback logic. Not recommended as primary owner.

Safety Services could own safety message prioritization and validation, but should not own the general packet loss mitigation architecture. Safety Services should interface with Teleoperation to tag and prioritize safety-critical messages, not own the communication pipeline. Not recommended as primary owner.

NGHE Solution is the system-level container but is too coarse-grained to own implementation details of packet loss mitigation. Ownership should be delegated to Teleoperation subsystem. Not recommended as primary owner.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Teleoperation module is the optimal owner for implementing DP-D (layered packet loss mitigation). Teleoperation owns the end-to-end communication pipeline and has clear visibility into both operator-side and vehicle-side communication requirements. Redundancy, QoS, FEC, and retransmission logic are all communication-layer concerns that naturally belong in Teleoperation. Interfaces to Network Link (for redundant channel access) and Safety Services (for safety message prioritization) are well-defined and minimal.
containment_risks: Risk: If Teleoperation module does not have direct access to Network Link abstraction for managing redundant channels, packet loss mitigation may be constrained. Mitigation: Ensure Network Link interface exposes redundancy management capabilities (e.g., channel selection, failover control).

Risk: If Safety Services does not provide clear message classification and prioritization guidance, Teleoperation may implement safety logic incorrectly. Mitigation: Establish a safety message tagging scheme and interface contract with Safety Services before detailed design.

Risk: If Vehicle module does not implement corresponding retransmission and error handling logic on the receive side, Teleoperation's mitigation efforts may be ineffective. Mitigation: Allocate corresponding sub-FRs to Vehicle module for reliable command reception and execution.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Confirm that Network Link interface supports redundant channel management (e.g., dual cellular, satellite backup) and that Safety Services provides a clear message classification and prioritization API. Define the boundary between Teleoperation's application-layer FEC/retransmission and any network-layer error handling in Network Link to avoid duplication or gaps. Allocate corresponding sub-FRs to Vehicle module for reliable command reception.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (layered packet loss mitigation) directly addresses FR-NGHE-009 (maintain packet loss ≤1%). The combination of redundancy, QoS, FEC, and retransmission is a proven approach in telecommunications and avionics for achieving low packet loss targets. DP-D is neither over-constrained nor under-constrained; it provides sufficient flexibility for implementation while maintaining clear traceability to the FR. Coupling between DP layers is manageable through interface contracts.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: If Network Link module does not support redundant channel management, Teleoperation may need to implement channel selection logic, increasing coupling and complexity.
Diffusion Risk 2: If Safety Services does not provide a clear message classification API, Teleoperation may need to embed safety domain knowledge, violating separation of concerns.
Diffusion Risk 3: If Vehicle module does not implement corresponding error handling on the receive side, Teleoperation's mitigation efforts may be incomplete, requiring rework.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation is the clear owner of DP-D implementation. Containment score of 95 indicates strong alignment. Diffusion is minimal (5%) and limited to well-defined interfaces (Network Link, Safety Services, Vehicle). Recommend establishing interface contracts with these modules before detailed design to minimize diffusion risks.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-009 is feasible through controlled network testing. Teleoperation module can expose communication quality metrics (packet loss, latency, jitter) via telemetry or diagnostic interfaces. Acceptance testing can measure end-to-end packet loss under representative network conditions and compare against customer-defined threshold (≤1%). Recommend establishing a test plan that covers nominal conditions, degraded network conditions, and failover scenarios.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of FR-NGHE-009 to Teleoperation module is clear. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-009.1 through 009.4) can be further allocated to specific components within Teleoperation (e.g., command transmission, telemetry reception, error recovery, monitoring). Recommend defining these sub-allocations in the next decomposition level.

recommended_revisions: Establish interface contracts with Network Link, Safety Services, and Vehicle modules to clarify responsibilities for redundancy, safety message prioritization, and error handling.
Define message classification scheme (safety-critical, control, telemetry, diagnostic) and ensure Safety Services provides prioritization guidance.
Specify latency budget allocation across redundancy switching, FEC decoding, and retransmission to ensure operator responsiveness requirements are met.
Develop a test plan for FR-NGHE-009 that covers nominal conditions, degraded network conditions, and failover scenarios. Define acceptance criteria for packet loss measurement.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-009 is not atomic and requires further decomposition. The refined FR identifies four sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-009.1 through 009.4) that address distinct aspects of teleoperation communication: command transmission, telemetry reception, error recovery, and monitoring. Each sub-FR should be decomposed to the next level to identify specific design parameters and implementation modules. Additionally, DP-D is a high-level architecture that requires detailed design decisions (redundancy strategy, FEC parameters, retransmission logic, message classification) that should be captured in the next decomposition level. The Teleoperation module assignment is sound and should be retained, but sub-module boundaries (e.g., command transmission subsystem, telemetry reception subsystem, error recovery subsystem) should be defined in the next level.
decision.justification_detail: The refined FR is complex and multi-faceted. While DP-D provides a sound architectural approach, the implementation details require further decomposition. Specifically: (1) FR-NGHE-009.1 (command transmission) requires design decisions about command encoding, transmission scheduling, and acknowledgment mechanisms. (2) FR-NGHE-009.2 (telemetry reception) requires decisions about telemetry buffering, prioritization, and delivery guarantees. (3) FR-NGHE-009.3 (error recovery) requires detailed specification of FEC codes, retransmission timeouts, and retry limits. (4) FR-NGHE-009.4 (monitoring) requires definition of metrics, reporting frequency, and diagnostic interfaces. Each of these sub-FRs should be decomposed independently to ensure complete and consistent design.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-009.1: The teleoperation system shall transmit operator control commands with reliable delivery to the remote vehicle.
FR-NGHE-009.2: The teleoperation system shall transmit vehicle telemetry and sensor data to the operator with reliable delivery.
FR-NGHE-009.3: The teleoperation system shall implement error detection and recovery mechanisms to minimize packet loss impact on safety-critical communications.
FR-NGHE-009.4: The teleoperation system shall monitor and report communication quality metrics (packet loss, latency) to enable operator awareness and system diagnostics.

assumptions: Customer-defined network conditions are stable and representative of actual deployment environment.
Redundant communication channels (e.g., dual cellular, satellite backup) are available and cost-effective.
Application-layer FEC and retransmission do not introduce unacceptable latency (assumed <200ms for heavy equipment teleoperation).
Safety-critical message classification is well-defined and stable.
Network Link module supports redundant channel management.
Safety Services module provides message classification and prioritization API.
Vehicle module implements corresponding error handling on the receive side.
open_risks: Risk: Actual field network conditions may differ from customer-defined conditions, making packet loss targets unachievable.
Risk: Redundant communication channels may not be available or cost-effective in all deployment regions.
Risk: Combined latency of redundancy switching, FEC decoding, and retransmission may exceed operator responsiveness requirements.
Risk: Safety message classification may be incomplete or incorrect, resulting in incorrect prioritization.
Risk: Network Link or Safety Services modules may not provide required interfaces or capabilities.
Risk: Vehicle module may not implement corresponding error handling, leaving teleoperation communication incomplete.
evidence_needed: Customer-defined network conditions and acceptance criteria (packet loss threshold, latency budget, network topology).
Availability and cost analysis of redundant communication channels for target deployment regions.
Latency budget allocation across redundancy, FEC, and retransmission layers.
Safety message classification scheme and prioritization guidance from Safety Services.
Interface specifications for Network Link (redundancy management), Safety Services (message prioritization), and Vehicle (error handling).
Test plan for FR-NGHE-009 covering nominal conditions, degraded network conditions, and failover scenarios.
Operator responsiveness requirements and latency tolerance for heavy equipment teleoperation.
